_doc: Allowlist of Google OAuth scopes this app requests via flask-dance
scripts/check_security_posture.py on a monthly cron to detect scope
drift — any new scope added to app.py that ISN'T in this list, or
any scope here that was REMOVED from app.py, fires a flag.
When you intentionally change the OAuth scopes:
3. Re-deploy. The cron's next run reads this file as source of
truth, so until you update it, the audit will flag the drift
Rationale for keeping the list small:
- openid + userinfo.email + userinfo.profile is enough to assert
'this is shigsdev@gmail.com' for the single-user lockdown.
- Adding read/write scopes on Calendar / Gmail / Drive would
massively expand the blast radius if the OAuth token were ever
stolen. Don't add unless absolutely needed.
